explanation: This question requires matching contestants with their respective Lok Sabha constituencies in the 2004 elections. Ale Narendra represented Medak, B. Vinod Kumar represented Adilabad, Ravindra Naik represented Warangal, and Madhusudhan Reddy represented Hanamkonda. Therefore, the correct option is A-IV, B-II, C-V, D-I.

The Girglani Commission was indeed appointed to study the issues of employees and assumed office on June 26th, 2001. Statement C is incorrect because it claims most departmental heads did not cooperate, whereas the commission's report indicated significant cooperation from most, with only a few exceptions.

The Antony Committee was formed to address the Telangana issue. Its members included Digvijay Singh, Ahmed Patel, P. Chidambaram, Janardan Dwivedi, and Narayan Swamy. This specific combination of individuals constitutes the correct answer.

The Pranab Mukherjee Sub-Committee was tasked with finding an agreement on the Telangana issue within the UPA government. The other members of this sub-committee were Dayanidhi Maran and Raghuvansh Prasad Singh, who were instrumental in the discussions.

Statement A is correct as K. Chandrashekar Rao did launch an indefinite hunger strike in New Delhi in 2005 for the cause of a separate Telangana. Statement B is incorrect because while Sharad Pawar was a minister, he did not give such an assurance to K. Chandrashekar Rao regarding the formation of Telangana State at that time.
The Group of Ministers (GOM) constituted for the creation of a separate Telangana State included Veerappa Moily, Jairam Ramesh, and Digvijay Singh. A.K. Antony was not a member of this specific GOM.

The incorrect pairs are A and B. The Telangana Atma Gaurava Sabha was held on December 12th, 2006, and the Samara Sankharavam was held on October 18th, 2006. The dates provided for these events in the question are correct, making the question's premise of identifying incorrect pairs flawed if these are the only options.

This question requires matching books with their respective publishers or writers. 'Kakati' is attributed to Gade Innaiah, 'Perspectives on Telangana' to Samidi Jagan Reddy, 'Telangana Lo Em Jarugutondi' to Telangana Information Trust, and 'Dagapadda Telangana' to K. Jayashankar. Thus, A-I, B-II, C-III, D-IV is the correct pairing.

The tragic deaths of approximately 2500 tribals in Adilabad district due to cholera in 1998 were brought to international attention through the efforts of Prof. Kesav Rao Jadav. His work ensured these deaths were published in the 'Wall Street Journal'.

The question asks to identify the incorrect pair. Without the specific pairs provided in the options, it's impossible to determine the incorrect one. However, assuming the options represent names of parties or events, one of them would be incorrectly associated or named.

The Telangana Mahasabha organized a one-day seminar at Suryapet with the title 'Dagapadda Telangana'. This title reflects the sentiment of betrayal or injustice felt by the Telangana region, which was the focus of the seminar.

Statement A is correct as Vajpayee declared BJP's support for Telangana during the 1997 election campaign. Statement B is also correct; Ale Narendra resigned from BJP in 1998 due to the party's inaction on bifurcation. Statement C is incomplete, but assuming it refers to a specific conference, the first two statements are factually accurate regarding the political landscape of the time.

The 'Muslim Garjana Sabha', organized by Jamat-e-Islam in support of Telangana, was presided over by Malik Mohasith Khan. He led the proceedings of this gathering which advocated for the formation of a separate state.

During the Telangana Movement, various forms of protest were observed across different regions. Caste associations in Medak held relay hunger strikes, Rangoli protests occurred at Siddipet, and at Akshapur, protestors prepared lunch on the roads, showcasing diverse methods of expressing dissent.

Statement A is correct as the CPI did initially support Samaikyandhra (United Andhra Pradesh). Statement B is also correct; CPI leader K. Narayana undertook a 'Jana Chaitanya Pada Yatra' to demand the formation of a separate Telangana state, indicating a shift in the party's stance.
